title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in consistent personal reader-state model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
Make set-state commands idempotent with explicit desired state, preconditions where needed, and stable response semantics.
Define bounded all-or-nothing or per-item bulk semantics, authorization, result reconciliation, and retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation
steps.instruction:

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2550 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.

lines.text: A displayed counter cannot drift if each command updates it.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Feed Go Count Reconciliation.
Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the feed go count reconciliation · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For feed go count reconciliation · fault isolation, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-count-reconciliation-8s2eoe" that uses case 8905 to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Go Count Reconciliation into observable performance evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation, keep Feed Go Count Reconciliation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Count Reconciliation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 3455 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.

For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A displayed counter cannot drift if each command updates it.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Feed Go Count Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Feed Go Count Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 7598 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.
